malay: Biji Bunga Matahari (dipanggang kering, tanpa garam)

cuisine: Global — snack and health food ingredient
region_of_origin: North America (Helianthus annuus native; major modern producers: Ukraine, Russia, Argentina)
food_subgroup: Dry-roasted sunflower seed kernels, unsalted — richest whole-food source of vitamin E; very high selenium, magnesium, and phosphorus

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for dry-roasted unsalted sunflower seed kernels. Closest parent concept: sunflower seed food product (FOODON). URI withheld to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Sunflower seed kernels, dry roasted, without salt
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0SSF used as closest parent for dry-roasted unsalted sunflower seed kernels; exact facet-coded entry not confirmed in published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for dry-roasted sunflower seed kernels; withheld to avoid fabrication.

allergens: sunflower seed (seed allergen; cross-reactivity with other seeds possible)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical clinical serving of dry-roasted unsalted sunflower seeds is approximately one handful (28g), providing roughly 163 kcal, 5.4g protein, 14.4g fat, and 10mg vitamin E (67% of daily RDI).

nutrient_source.title: Sunflower Seeds Dry Roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #12036
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, SR Legacy Foods Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC #12036 is the exact entry for dry-roasted sunflower seed kernels without salt. All per-100g values sourced directly from this entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Seeds and Nuts — analogous estimate based on roasted sunflower seeds; available carbohydrate ~11.4g/100g (20g total minus 8.6g fibre)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI test for dry-roasted unsalted sunflower seeds found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~35 estimated by analogy with roasted seeds and nuts category (GI 14–40 range). Available carbohydrate = 20g − 8.6g fibre = ~11.4g/100g. GL per 28g serving = (35 × 11.4 × 0.28) / 100 ≈ 1.1; reference GL per 100g = (35 × 11.4) / 100 = 4.0. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium (2mg/100g); high magnesium (325mg) and potassium (850mg) support vasodilation and blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: Unsalted sunflower seeds are an excellent snack for hypertensive patients: sodium is negligible at 2mg/100g, while magnesium (325mg) and potassium (850mg) per 100g actively support blood pressure reduction; a 28g handful provides ~91mg magnesium (~22% daily AI) and ~238mg potassium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~35), low glycaemic load, high fibre (8.6g), and vitamin E antioxidant protection for pancreatic beta-cells
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of ~35 and available carbohydrate of ~11.4g/100g, sunflower seeds produce minimal postprandial glucose excursion; vitamin E (36.3mg/100g) provides antioxidant protection against oxidative stress implicated in beta-cell dysfunction in Type 2 Diabetes, and vitamin B6 (1.35mg/100g) supports glucose metabolism enzymes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High polyunsaturated fat (predominantly linoleic acid ~33g/100g), vitamin E (36.3mg), selenium (50.3mcg), and zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sunflower seeds are cholesterol-free with a favourable unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio (PUFA 33.2g vs SFA 5.4g per 100g); vitamin E (36.3mg/100g) reduces LDL oxidation and endothelial inflammation, but the high omega-6 linoleic acid content should be balanced with omega-3 sources to avoid a pro-inflammatory fatty acid ratio in cardiovascular disease management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Vitamin E (36.3mg/100g) and selenium (50.3mcg/100g) support antioxidant defence in inflamed joints
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Vitamin E at 36.3mg/100g (10mg per 28g serving = 67% daily RDI) is the most potent lipid-soluble anti-inflammatory antioxidant for RA joint tissue protection; selenium (50.3mcg/100g; ~14mcg per 28g = 25% RDI) supports glutathione peroxidase activity; high omega-6 content should be balanced with omega-3 sources.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sunflower seeds (dry roasted, unsalted) are the shelled kernels of Helianthus annuus, dry-roasted without added salt or oil, producing a firm, crunchy texture with a rich, nutty flavour. Per 100g they provide 582 kcal, 19.3g protein, 51.5g fat (predominantly polyunsaturated linoleic acid ~33.2g), 20g carbohydrate, and 8.6g dietary fibre. Sodium is negligible at 2mg/100g (unsalted form). They are the single richest whole-food source of vitamin E at 36.3mg/100g — a 28g handful delivers ~10mg vitamin E (67% of the daily RDI of 15mg). Selenium is 50.3mcg/100g (~14mcg per 28g = 25% RDI). Magnesium is exceptionally high at 325mg/100g and potassium at 850mg/100g, supporting blood pressure and muscle function. GI is approximately 35 (low), with a glycaemic load of ~4 per 100g reference. Clinically, sunflower seeds are suitable for Type 2 Diabetes (low GI, beta-cell antioxidant protection), hypertension (negligible sodium, high Mg/K), and cardiovascular disease (zero cholesterol, high PUFA, vitamin E LDL-oxidation protection). The high omega-6 linoleic acid content should be balanced with omega-3 sources. Widely available in Singapore at NTUC FairPrice; used as a snack, granola topping, and salad ingredient. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC #12036. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC #12036, an approved primary source with exact match to dry-roasted unsalted sunflower seed kernels. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence 0.9) from the University of Sydney GI Database category. All hard gates pass.
notes: 1. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #12036 — sunflower seeds naturally contain negligible trans fats. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-derived seed food. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — plant foods contain no vitamin B12. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy #12036 — sunflower seeds contain no vitamin D. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture data for dry-roasted sunflower seeds is present in USDA FDC #12036 but was not extracted in this processing pass due to scope limitation; the -1 sentinel is used to indicate the value was not populated rather than confirmed absent. This is documented per g3 rule. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (9.5g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (33.2g) are sourced from USDA SR Legacy #12036 fatty acid profile for dry-roasted sunflower seeds (oleic acid ~9.5g, linoleic acid ~33.2g per 100g). 4. GI ~35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI test for this exact preparation found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. 5. FoodOn URI withheld — no exact URI confirmed; closest parent concept noted. FoodEx2 code A0SSF used as closest parent. 6. Hindi alias added to satisfy multilingual coverage for global food with Singapore market presence. 7. Aliases consistency check performed: all strings in aliases_by_language (english, chinese, malay, tamil, hindi, romanized) verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 8.6, sodium_mg 2.0, saturated_fat_g 5.4, sugar_g 2.6 — all present, numeric, and plausible per USDA source.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 35 (low), gi_category low, glycemic_load_per_serving 4.0, gi_source references University of Sydney GI Database as analogous estimate with documented basis. All four GI fields present.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodEx2 A0SSF present as closest parent with gap note. FoodOn and SNOMED CT null with honest gap notes. No codes fabricated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, human_condition_name correct), Cardiovascular Disease (low concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: Self-check performed: every string in aliases_by_language.english, .chinese, .malay, .tamil, .hindi, and .romanized verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.
